utilities.note: electric + water + internet; USD-dollarized economy; mild highland climate

groceries.note: home cooking; mercados exceptional value; USD pricing = predictable

diningOut.note: almuerzos $2.50–$3.50 for 3-course lunch; best value in Ecuador

transit.note: buses + taxis; city walkable; very low transport costs

simData.note: Claro/Movistar 30-day plan; USD = no FX risk

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Cuenca is very flat seasonally.

evidence.quote: A single person costs $1,060 per month; for a single expat, Cuenca costs $1,000–1,500/month
Rent ranges from $350–550 for a 1-bedroom furnished apartment. A furnished 2-bedroom costs $650-900

evidence.claim: 3-course almuerzo $2.50–$3.50; best food deal in Ecuador
evidence.quote: A full three-course lunch — soup, main plate with protein, rice, salad, and juice — costs $2.50-$3.50 at local restaurants, and this is the best deal in the entire country

evidence.claim: one expat living on $750/month in Cuenca with full breakdown
evidence.quote: Cost of Living in Ecuador 2026: I Spend $750/mo in Cuenca (Full Breakdown)
